displayTitle: Five Answers, On Paper, Every Time

beats.narration: A Level One runs the technique they're handed. A Level Two builds it: five answers before current flows through an unfamiliar part.

payload.title: BUILD THE PLAN
payload.sub: From unknown part to written, defensible technique.

beats.narration: Five questions, in order. What is the part: alloy, shape, condition. What current. Which directions. What sequence. And how much field.
Question one is the part itself. Alloy sets continuous or residual. Shape picks the hardware. Condition finishes the call: dry powder or wet bath.
Question five. Formulas are starting points; proof finishes the answer. The Q Q I shim lights up, about thirty to sixty gauss on the meter.

payload.heading: Set by formula, proven on the part

beats.narration: The rule: five answers, on paper, every time. That's a technique you can defend to any auditor. Skip one, and you're guessing.

payload.sub: Answer all five — or you're guessing.
